axiom Animal : Type
axiom Money : Type

axiom Tom : Person

axiom AllowsPets : Building → Prop
axiom Pet : Animal → Prop
axiom Cat : Animal → Prop
axiom Owns : Person → Animal → Prop

axiom Rent : Building → Money
axiom Deposit : Building → Money

axiom More : Money → Money → Prop
axiom AtMost : Money → Money → Prop

axiom WillRent : Person → Building → Prop
axiom DepositRequired : Building → Prop

axiom S2 : ∀ b : Building, Managed b → ∀ p : Person, WillRent p b → DepositRequired b

axiom S4a : Cat Fluffy
axiom S4b : Owns Tom Fluffy
axiom S5 : ∀ a : Animal, Cat a → Pet a

axiom S9 : ∀ b : Building, Managed b → (AllowedWithPet b Tom Fluffy ∧ AtMost (Deposit b) M1500) → WillRent Tom b

theorem stmt_true : Statement :=

  cases h with

        simpa [S7] using hEq
      exact Or.inl hEq'
  | inr hMore =>
      have hMore' : More (Deposit OliveGarden) M2000 := by
        simpa [S7] using hMore
      exact Or.inr hMore'
</lean>

<lean>
axiom Show : Type
axiom ST : Show
axiom BM : Show

axiom Netflix : Show → Prop
axiom Popular : Show → Prop
axiom BingeK : Show → Prop
axiom DownloadK : Show → Prop
axiom ShareWithLisa : Show → Prop

-- Premises
axiom ST_popular : Popular ST
axiom ST_netflix : Netflix ST
axiom popular_netflix_implies_binge :
  ∀ s : Show, (Netflix s ∧ Popular s) → BingeK s
-- Interpreting "iff Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will download it"
-- as: for any Netflix show s, she downloads s iff there exists some Netflix show she binge-watches
axiom download_iff_exists_binged_netflix :
  ∀ s : Show, Netflix s → (DownloadK s ↔ ∃ t : Show, Netflix t ∧ BingeK t)
axiom BM_not_download : ¬DownloadK BM
axiom BM_netflix : Netflix BM
axiom binge_implies_share_with_lisa :
  ∀ s : Show, Netflix s → (BingeK s → ShareWithLisa s)

theorem not_share_ST : ¬ShareWithLisa ST := by
  intro _hshare
  -- From "Stranger Things" is a popular Netflix show and the rule,
  -- Karen binge-watches ST.
  have binge_ST : BingeK ST := by
    have h : Netflix ST ∧ Popular ST := And.intro ST_netflix ST_popular
    exact popular_netflix_implies_binge ST h
  -- Therefore, there exists a Netflix show Karen binge-watches (namely, ST).
  have exists_binged : ∃ t : Show, Netflix t ∧ BingeK t :=
    Exists.intro ST (And.intro ST_netflix binge_ST)
  -- By the iff premise applied to BM (a Netflix show), she downloads BM.
  have hiff : DownloadK BM ↔ ∃ t : Show, Netflix t ∧ BingeK t :=
    download_iff_exists_binged_netflix BM BM_netflix
  have dlBM : DownloadK BM := (Iff.mpr hiff) exists_binged
  -- Contradiction with "Karen does not download BM".
  exact BM_not_download dlBM
</lean>

ANSWER: False

    have hNotHuman : ¬Human Marvin := A3 Marvin hET
    have hEarth : FromEarth Marvin := And.left hBoth
    have hHuman : Human Marvin := A4 Marvin (And.intro A5 hEarth)

  -- Use the exclusive-style disjunction to deduce the "neither" case.

axiom LiveWithStrangers : obj → Prop

axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, SiblingsBornTogether x → MultipleBirth x

  exact hNotS hS

theorem luke_not_with_strangers : ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke := by
  have h2 : ¬Baby Luke ∧ ¬MultipleBirth Luke ∧ ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke :=

  exact And.right (And.right h2)

  have h2 : ¬Baby Luke ∧ ¬MultipleBirth Luke ∧ ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke :=

axiom HaveSiblingsBornTogether : obj → Prop
axiom ComplainOften : obj → Prop
axiom LiveAtHome : obj → Prop
axiom LiveWithStrangers : obj → Prop

-- People who are born in a multiple birth with siblings spend a lot of time with their siblings.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, MultipleBirth x → SpendTimeWithSiblings x
-- If people have siblings who were born together, then they were born in a multiple birth.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, HaveSiblingsBornTogether x → MultipleBirth x
-- If people complain often about annoying siblings, then they have siblings who were born together.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, ComplainOften x → HaveSiblingsBornTogether x
-- If people live at home, then they do not live with strangers.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, LiveAtHome x → ¬LiveWithStrangers x
-- If people spend a lot of time with their siblings, then they often live at home.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, SpendTimeWithSiblings x → LiveAtHome x
-- Luke either is born in a multiple birth and lives with strangers, or is not born in a multiple birth and does not live with strangers.
axiom Luke_case : (MultipleBirth Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) ∨ (¬MultipleBirth Luke ∧ ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke)

-- From being born in a multiple birth, one cannot live with strangers.
theorem mult_implies_not_strangers : ∀ x : obj, MultipleBirth x → ¬LiveWithStrangers x := by
  intro x
  intro hM
  have hST : SpendTimeWithSiblings x := R1 x hM
  have hHome : LiveAtHome x := R5 x hST
  exact R4 x hHome

-- Luke is neither born in a multiple birth nor complains often about annoying siblings.
theorem luke_neither_multiple_nor_complains : ¬MultipleBirth Luke ∧ ¬ComplainOften Luke := by
  -- Eliminate the impossible first disjunct (MultipleBirth ∧ LiveWithStrangers).
  have not_first : ¬(MultipleBirth Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) := by
    intro h
    have hM : MultipleBirth Luke := And.left h
    have hS : LiveWithStrangers Luke := And.right h
    have hnS : ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke := mult_implies_not_strangers Luke hM
    exact hnS hS
  have second : ¬MultipleBirth Luke ∧ ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke := Or.resolve_left Luke_case not_first
  have notM : ¬MultipleBirth Luke := And.left second
  -- Complains → MultipleBirth
  have comp_to_mult : ∀ x : obj, ComplainOften x → MultipleBirth x := by
    intro x
    intro hC
    have hTogether : HaveSiblingsBornTogether x := R3 x hC
    exact R2 x hTogether
  -- Hence ¬Complains from ¬MultipleBirth
  have notC : ¬ComplainOften Luke := by
    intro hC
    have hM : MultipleBirth Luke := comp_to_mult Luke hC
    exact notM hM
  exact And.intro notM notC
</lean>

axiom BornInMultiple : obj → Prop
axiom SpendTimeWithSiblings : obj → Prop
axiom HaveSiblingsBornTogether : obj → Prop
axiom ComplainsOftenAboutAnnoyingSiblings : obj → Prop
axiom LiveAtHome : obj → Prop
axiom LiveWithStrangers : obj → Prop

-- People who are born in a multiple birth spend time with their siblings
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, BornInMultiple x → SpendTimeWithSiblings x
-- If people have siblings who were born together, then they were born in a multiple birth
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, HaveSiblingsBornTogether x → BornInMultiple x
-- If people complain often about annoying siblings, then they have siblings who were born together
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, ComplainsOftenAboutAnnoyingSiblings x → HaveSiblingsBornTogether x
-- If people live at home, then they do not live with strangers
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, LiveAtHome x → ¬LiveWithStrangers x
-- If people spend a lot of time with their siblings, then they live at home
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, SpendTimeWithSiblings x → LiveAtHome x
-- Luke either is born in a multiple birth and lives with strangers, or is not born in a multiple birth and does not live with strangers
axiom Luke_cases : (BornInMultiple Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) ∨ (¬BornInMultiple Luke ∧ ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke)

theorem luke_not_complain : ¬ComplainsOftenAboutAnnoyingSiblings Luke := by
  intro hC
  have hHS : HaveSiblingsBornTogether Luke := A3 Luke hC
  have hBorn : BornInMultiple Luke := A2 Luke hHS
  have hSpend : SpendTimeWithSiblings Luke := A1 Luke hBorn
  have hHome : LiveAtHome Luke := A5 Luke hSpend
  have hNotStr : ¬LiveWithStrangers Luke := A4 Luke hHome
  have hStr : LiveWithStrangers Luke := by
    cases Luke_cases with
    | inl h =>
      exact h.right
    | inr h =>
      have hFalse : False := h.left hBorn
      exact False.elim hFalse
  exact hNotStr hStr
</lean>

ANSWER: False

axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ Adores x → ¬ Interesting x

-- 4. All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are evocative.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, KellyStyle x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x

-- 5. All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are dreamy.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, KellyStyle x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x

axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

  intro x

  have h_design : DesignByMax x := And.left h
  have h_ad_zaha : Adores x ∧ ZahaStyle x := And.right h
  have h_ador : Adores x := And.left h_ad_zaha
  have h_zaha : ZahaStyle x := And.right h_ad_zaha
  -- ZahaStyle ∧ Adores ⇒ Interesting
  have h_int : Interesting x := A1 x (And.intro h_zaha h_ador)
  -- DesignByMax ∧ Adores ∧ Interesting ⇒ Brutalist ∧ Evocative
  have h_b_ev : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := A6 x (And.intro h_design (And.intro h_ador h_int))
  have h_brut : Brutalist x := And.left h_b_ev
  -- Brutalist ∧ Adores ⇒ ¬Interesting
  have h_not_int : ¬ Interesting x := A2 x (And.intro h_brut h_ador)
  exact h_not_int h_int
</lean>

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

axiom DesignByMax : obj → Prop
axiom Adored : obj → Prop
axiom Zaha : obj → Prop
axiom Kelly : obj → Prop
axiom Interesting : obj → Prop
axiom Brutalist : obj → Prop
axiom Evocative : obj → Prop
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop

-- All of Zaha Hadid's design styles that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Zaha x ∧ Adored x → Interesting x
-- No brutalist buildings that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ Adored x → ¬ Interesting x
-- Every style that Max adores is either Zaha Hadid's design style or Kelly Wearstler's design style.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Adored x → (Zaha x ∨ Kelly x)
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are evocative.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adored x → Evocative x
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are dreamy.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adored x → Dreamy x
-- If a design by Max that he adores has interesting geometries, then it is a brutalist building and evocative.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adored x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- Useful consequences of the premises.

-- No design by Max that he adores can be a Zaha design (since that would force Interesting and ¬Interesting).
theorem no_zaha_design_by_max_adored : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adored x ∧ Zaha x → False := by
  intro x
  intro h
  have dmx : DesignByMax x := And.left h
  have had : Adored x := And.left (And.right h)
  have hz  : Zaha x := And.right (And.right h)
  have hi  : Interesting x := A1 x (And.intro hz had)
  have hbe : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := A6 x (And.intro dmx (And.intro had hi))
  have hb  : Brutalist x := And.left hbe
  have hni : ¬ Interesting x := A2 x (And.intro hb had)
  exact hni hi

-- Any design by Max that he adores must be a Kelly design.
theorem adored_design_by_max_is_kelly : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adored x → Kelly x := by
  intro x
  intro h
  have had : Adored x := And.right h
  have disj : Zaha x ∨ Kelly x := A3 x had
  cases disj with
  | inl hz =>
      have contra : False := no_zaha_design_by_max_adored x (And.intro (And.left h) (And.intro had hz))
      exact False.elim contra
  | inr hk =>
      exact hk

-- Any design by Max that he adores is both evocative and dreamy.
theorem adored_design_by_max_evocative_dreamy : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adored x → Evocative x ∧ Dreamy x := by
  intro x
  intro h
  have had : Adored x := And.right h
  have hk  : Kelly x := adored_design_by_max_is_kelly x h
  have hev : Evocative x := A4 x (And.intro hk had)
  have hdr : Dreamy x := A5 x (And.intro hk had)
  exact And.intro hev hdr

-- No design by Max that he adores can have interesting geometries.
theorem no_adored_interesting_design_by_max : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adored x ∧ Interesting x → False := by
  intro x
  intro h
  have dmx : DesignByMax x := And.left h
  have had : Adored x := And.left (And.right h)
  have hi  : Interesting x := And.right (And.right h)
  have hbe : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := A6 x (And.intro dmx (And.intro had hi))
  have hb  : Brutalist x := And.left hbe
  have hni : ¬ Interesting x := A2 x (And.intro hb had)
  exact hni hi

-- The statement in question (for reference):
def Statement : Prop := ∃ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Evocative x ∧ Dreamy x
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

axiom Evocative : obj → Prop

axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ Adores x → ¬Interesting x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Adores x → (Zaha x ∨ Kelly x)
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, ByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- Some consequences we can prove from the premises

theorem no_bymax_adored_zaha : ∀ x : obj, ByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Zaha x → False :=
fun x h =>
  have hBy : ByMax x := h.left
  have hAdZa : Adores x ∧ Zaha x := h.right
  have hAd : Adores x := hAdZa.left
  have hZa : Zaha x := hAdZa.right
  have hInt : Interesting x := P1 x (And.intro hZa hAd)
  have hBrEv : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := P6 x (And.intro hBy (And.intro hAd hInt))
  have hBr : Brutalist x := hBrEv.left
  have hNotInt : ¬Interesting x := P2 x (And.intro hBr hAd)
  show False from hNotInt hInt

theorem bymax_adored_implies_kelly : ∀ x : obj, ByMax x ∧ Adores x → Kelly x :=
fun x h =>

axiom obj : Type

axiom Coco : obj
axiom RG : obj → Prop
axiom Female : obj → Prop
axiom Male : obj → Prop

axiom LostRafa : obj → Prop
axiom RankedHigh : obj → Prop
axiom MostActive : obj → Prop

-- If someone is ranked highly by the WTA, then they are one of the most active players in major tennis.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, RankedHigh x → MostActive x
-- Everyone who lost to Iga Świątek at Roland Garros 2022 is ranked highly by the WTA.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, LostIga x → RankedHigh x
-- All female tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Iga Świątek.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, RG x ∧ Female x → LostIga x
-- All tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 are either female or male.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, RG x → Female x ∨ Male x
-- All male tennis players at Roland Garros in 2022 lost to Rafael Nadal.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, RG x ∧ Male x → LostRafa x
-- If Coco Gauff is ranked highly by the WTA or lost to Rafael Nadal, then Coco Gauff is not a male tennis player at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom A6 : (RankedHigh Coco ∨ LostRafa Coco) → ¬ (RG Coco ∧ Male Coco)
-- Coco Gauff is at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom A7 : RG Coco

-- We prove the negation of the statement to evaluate:
-- Statement to evaluate: ¬ (LostIga Coco ∧ MostActive Coco)
-- Negation: LostIga Coco ∧ MostActive Coco
theorem coco_lostIga_and_mostActive : LostIga Coco ∧ MostActive Coco := by
  have hRG : RG Coco := A7
  -- First, show Coco is not male.
  have not_male : ¬ Male Coco := by
    intro m
    have lr : LostRafa Coco := A5 Coco ⟨hRG, m⟩
    have disj : RankedHigh Coco ∨ LostRafa Coco := Or.inr lr
    have hnot : ¬ (RG Coco ∧ Male Coco) := A6 disj
    exact hnot ⟨hRG, m⟩
  -- From RG and the female-or-male axiom plus not_male, conclude Female Coco.
  have fem_or_male : Female Coco ∨ Male Coco := A4 Coco hRG
  have fem : Female Coco := Or.elim fem_or_male (fun f => f) (fun m => False.elim (not_male m))
  -- Hence Coco lost to Iga at RG 2022.
  have li : LostIga Coco := A3 Coco ⟨hRG, fem⟩
  -- Therefore Coco is ranked highly.
  have rh : RankedHigh Coco := A2 Coco li
  -- Therefore Coco is one of the most active players in major tennis.
  have ma : MostActive Coco := A1 Coco rh
  exact And.intro li ma
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

axiom Man : obj → Prop
axiom InMClass : obj → Prop
axiom Taller : obj → obj → Prop
axiom CanBlock : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Jumps : obj → Prop

axiom Great : obj → Prop

axiom Michael : obj
axiom Peter : obj
axiom Windy : obj

-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.
axiom R1 : ∀ x y : obj, Man x → Man y → Taller x y → CanBlock x y

-- Michael is a man who is taller than everyone else in his class.
axiom Michael_is_man : Man Michael
axiom Michael_taller_class : ∀ z : obj, InMClass z ∧ z ≠ Michael → Taller Michael z

-- Transitivity of taller
axiom taller_trans : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom Peter_is_man : Man Peter
axiom Peter_taller_than_Michael : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom Michael_blocks_if_not_jump : ∀ p : obj, ¬Jumps p → CanBlock Michael p

-- Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom not_Michael_blocks_Windy : ¬CanBlock Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom great_shooter : ∀ x : obj, Shooter x ∧ Jumps x → Great x

-- Natural property of "taller than": irreflexivity
axiom taller_irrefl : ∀ x : obj, ¬Taller x x

-- Statement to evaluate (negation proved): Peter is NOT shorter than a man in Michael's class.
-- That is, there does not exist a man in Michael's class who is taller than Peter.
theorem peter_not_shorter_than_classman :
  ¬(∃ m : obj, Man m ∧ InMClass m ∧ Taller m Peter) := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | intro m hm =>
    have hIn_T : InMClass m ∧ Taller m Peter := And.right hm
    have hIn : InMClass m := And.left hIn_T
    have hmPeter : Taller m Peter := And.right hIn_T
    -- From m > Peter and Peter > Michael, get m > Michael
    have hmMichael : Taller m Michael :=
      taller_trans m Peter Michael hmPeter Peter_taller_than_Michael
    -- Hence m ≠ Michael (otherwise we'd get Michael > Michael)
    have hneq : m ≠ Michael := by
      intro heq
      have : Taller Michael Michael := by
        simpa [heq] using hmMichael
      exact (taller_irrefl Michael) this
    -- Michael is taller than every other member of his class (including m since m ≠ Michael)
    have hMichaelm : Taller Michael m :=
      Michael_taller_class m (And.intro hIn hneq)
    -- Compose to get Michael > Michael, contradiction
    have : Taller Michael Michael :=
      taller_trans Michael m Michael hMichaelm hmMichael
    exact (taller_irrefl Michael) this
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

axiom Iga : obj
axiom Nadal : obj

axiom LostTo : obj → obj → Prop
axiom AtRG2022 : obj → Prop
axiom Female : obj → Prop
axiom Male : obj → Prop

-- Axioms from the text
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, RankedWTA x → MostActiveMajor x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, AtRG2022 x ∧ LostTo x Iga → RankedWTA x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, AtRG2022 x ∧ Female x → LostTo x Iga
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, AtRG2022 x → Female x ∨ Male x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, AtRG2022 x ∧ Male x → LostTo x Nadal
axiom A6 : (RankedWTA Coco ∨ LostTo Coco Nadal) → ¬(Male Coco ∧ AtRG2022 Coco)
axiom A7 : AtRG2022 Coco

theorem coco_not_male_if_lost_nadal : LostTo Coco Nadal → ¬Male Coco := by
  intro hLost
  have hnot : ¬(Male Coco ∧ AtRG2022 Coco) := A6 (Or.inr hLost)
  have hAt : AtRG2022 Coco := A7
  intro hMale
  apply hnot
  exact And.intro hMale hAt

theorem coco_female_if_lost_nadal : LostTo Coco Nadal → Female Coco := by
  intro hLost
  have hAt : AtRG2022 Coco := A7
  have hOr : Female Coco ∨ Male Coco := A4 Coco hAt
  have hNotMale : ¬Male Coco := coco_not_male_if_lost_nadal hLost
  exact Or.elim hOr (fun hF => hF) (fun hM => False.elim (hNotMale hM))

theorem coco_lost_to_iga_if_lost_nadal : LostTo Coco Nadal → LostTo Coco Iga := by
  intro hLost
  have hF : Female Coco := coco_female_if_lost_nadal hLost
  have hAt : AtRG2022 Coco := A7
  exact A3 Coco (And.intro hAt hF)

theorem coco_ranked_if_lost_nadal : LostTo Coco Nadal → RankedWTA Coco := by
  intro hLost
  have hIga : LostTo Coco Iga := coco_lost_to_iga_if_lost_nadal hLost
  have hAt : AtRG2022 Coco := A7
  exact A2 Coco (And.intro hAt hIga)

theorem coco_most_active_if_lost_nadal : LostTo Coco Nadal → MostActiveMajor Coco := by
  intro hLost
  have hRank : RankedWTA Coco := coco_ranked_if_lost_nadal hLost
  exact A1 Coco hRank
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

axiom obj : Type
axiom Jane : obj
axiom KiKi : obj

axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Barks : obj → Prop
axiom Dog : obj → Prop
axiom Jumps : obj → Prop
axiom Legs : obj → Prop
axiom Terricolous : obj → Prop
axiom Likes : obj → obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Barks x → ¬Likes Jane x
-- All dogs are animals that bark.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → Animal x ∧ Barks x
-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Jumps x → Likes Jane x
-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Legs x → Jumps x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Terricolous x → Legs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬Legs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi)

-- From legs to jumps for KiKi.
theorem legs_implies_jumps_KiKi : Legs KiKi → Jumps KiKi :=
by
  intro hL
  have h := A4 KiKi
  exact h (And.intro A6 hL)

-- From (¬J ∧ ¬L) derive L for KiKi, using terricolous→legs.
theorem conj_implies_legs_KiKi : (¬Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬Legs KiKi) → Legs KiKi :=
by
  intro h
  have hTL := A7 h
  cases hTL with
  | inl hT =>
      have h' := A5 KiKi
      exact h' (And.intro A6 hT)
  | inr hL =>
      exact hL

-- Therefore, not both (¬J ∧ ¬L) for KiKi.
theorem not_both_notJ_notL_KiKi : ¬(¬Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬Legs KiKi) :=
by
  intro h
  have hL := conj_implies_legs_KiKi h
  exact h.right hL

-- Hence ¬¬Jumps KiKi (constructively).
theorem notnot_jumps_KiKi : ¬¬Jumps KiKi :=
by
  intro hNotJ
  have hNotL : ¬Legs KiKi := fun hL => hNotJ (legs_implies_jumps_KiKi hL)
  have hConj : (¬Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬Legs KiKi) := And.intro hNotJ hNotL
  exact not_both_notJ_notL_KiKi hConj
</lean>

  intro x hx
  have hio : InviteOthers x := A3 x hx
  have hmp : MakePlans x := A2 x hio

  have hdisj : WellAttended John ∨ InviteFriends John := A6 hLF
  cases hdisj with

      have hIO : InviteOthers John := A3 John hWA
      have hMP : MakePlans John := A2 John hIO
      exact hNotMP hMP
  | inr hIF =>
      exact hNotIF hIF

    exact hNotMP hMP

  have hs : Suitable KO := KO_suitable
  have hns : ¬Suitable KO := A2 KO hb
  exact hns hs

  intro hg
  have hb : Bought KO := A1 KO hg

by
  intro h
  have hns : ¬Stock KO := And.left h
  have hs : Stock KO := KOstock

  have hns : ¬Suitable KO := A2 KO hb
  have hs : Suitable KO := KO_suitable

-- because its antecedent is refutable:

by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | inl hg =>
      have hb : Bought KO := A1 KO hg
      exact KO_not_bought hb
  | inr hb =>
      exact KO_not_bought hb

axiom RemoteFromHome : obj → Prop

axiom AppearsInCompanyToday : obj → Prop

-- 1. All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → SchedulesMeetings x → GoesToBuildingToday x
-- 2. Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, LunchInCompany x → SchedulesMeetings x
-- 3. Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → (LunchInCompany x ∨ LunchAtHome x)
-- 4. If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → LunchAtHome x → RemoteFromHome x
-- 5. All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → InOtherCountries x → RemoteFromHome x
-- 6. No managers work remotely from home.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, Manager x → ¬RemoteFromHome x

axiom A7 : AppearsInCompanyToday James ↔ Manager James

  Manager James → Employee James → LunchInCompany James := by
  intro jm je
  have disj := A3 James je
  cases disj with
  | inl linco =>
      exact linco
  | inr lhome =>
      have rfh := A4 James je lhome
      have nrfh := A6 James jm
      exact False.elim (nrfh rfh)

theorem james_lunch_in_company_if_appears_and_employee :
  Employee James → AppearsInCompanyToday James → LunchInCompany James := by
  intro je ja
  have jm : Manager James := (Iff.mp A7) ja
  exact james_lunch_in_company_if_manager_and_employee jm je
</lean>

axiom Animal : obj → Prop

axiom Tired : obj → Prop
axiom Flu : obj → Prop

-- Premises
-- When the Monkeypox virus occurs in a being, it gets Monkeypox.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, VirusOccurs x → Monkeypox x

axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Monkeypox x → Fever x ∧ Headache x ∧ MusclePains x ∧ Tired x
-- Humans are mammals.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Human x → Mammal x

axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Mammal x → Animal x
-- Monkeypox virus can occur in certain animals (existence).
axiom P5 : ∃ x : obj, Animal x ∧ VirusOccurs x
-- People feel tired when they get the flu.
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Human x ∧ Flu x → Tired x

-- The statement to evaluate: "No one gets the flu."
def NoOneGetsFlu : Prop := ∀ x : obj, ¬ Flu x

-- Derivable but unrelated to flu: there exists someone tired (from Monkeypox in some animal)
lemma exists_tired : ∃ x : obj, Tired x :=
by
  cases P5 with
  | intro a ha =>
    cases ha with
    | intro haAnimal hOcc =>
      have hMp : Monkeypox a := P1 a hOcc
      have hSym := P2 a hMp
      cases hSym with
      | intro hF rest1 =>
        cases rest1 with
        | intro hH rest2 =>
          cases rest2 with
          | intro hM hT =>
            exact ⟨a, hT⟩
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

axiom Mixture : obj

axiom Hydrocarbon : obj → Prop
axiom Organic : obj → Prop
axiom Chemical : obj → Prop
axiom ContainsCarbon : obj → Prop
axiom OnlyOneElement : obj → Prop
axiom Alkane : obj → Prop

axiom H_to_O : ∀ x : obj, Hydrocarbon x → Organic x
axiom A_to_H : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Hydrocarbon x
axiom O_to_Chem : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → Chemical x
axiom O_to_Carbon : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → ContainsCarbon x
axiom No_Chem_OnlyOne : ∀ x : obj, Chemical x → ¬OnlyOneElement x
axiom Mix_either_both_or_neither :
  (Chemical Mixture ∧ OnlyOneElement Mixture) ∨ (¬Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬OnlyOneElement Mixture)

-- Derived: Alkanes are chemical compounds
theorem alkane_implies_chemical : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Chemical x := by
  intro x
  intro hx
  have hh : Hydrocarbon x := A_to_H x hx
  have ho : Organic x := H_to_O x hh
  exact O_to_Chem x ho

-- From the "either both or neither" plus "no chemical compounds are single-element",
-- we get that the mixture is neither a chemical compound nor single-element.
theorem mixture_not_chemical_and_not_one : ¬Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬OnlyOneElement Mixture := by
  cases Mix_either_both_or_neither with
  | inl hboth =>
      have contra : False := by
        have hchem : Chemical Mixture := And.left hboth
        have hone : OnlyOneElement Mixture := And.right hboth
        have hnotone : ¬OnlyOneElement Mixture := No_Chem_OnlyOne Mixture hchem
        exact hnotone hone
      exact False.elim contra
  | inr hneither =>
      exact hneither

-- Hence the mixture is not an alkane (since alkanes are chemical compounds).
theorem mixture_not_alkane : ¬Alkane Mixture := by
  intro halk
  have hchem : Chemical Mixture := alkane_implies_chemical Mixture halk
  have hnotchem : ¬Chemical Mixture := And.left mixture_not_chemical_and_not_one
  exact hnotchem hchem

-- The given statement actually holds from the above facts.
theorem statement_holds :
  (OnlyOneElement Mixture ∨ ContainsCarbon Mixture) → (¬Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬Alkane Mixture) := by
  intro _
  have hnotchem : ¬Chemical Mixture := And.left mixture_not_chemical_and_not_one
  have hnotalk : ¬Alkane Mixture := mixture_not_alkane
  exact And.intro hnotchem hnotalk
</lean>

  | inr h2 =>

  have hCup : Cupcake DriedThaiChilies := R4 DriedThaiChilies hF
  have hBaked : BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies := R2 DriedThaiChilies hCup
  exact dtc_not_baked hBaked

-- The statement to evaluate: Dried Thai chilies are neither products of BBM nor baked sweets.
theorem statement_true : ¬FromBBM DriedThaiChilies ∧ ¬BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies :=
  And.intro dtc_not_bbm dtc_not_baked

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Entities
axiom Rockie : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Rabbit : obj → Prop
axiom Squirrel : obj → Prop
axiom Turtle : obj → Prop
axiom Spotted : obj → Prop
axiom Cute : obj → Prop
axiom Skittish : obj → Prop
axiom Calm : obj → Prop

-- Premises
-- All rabbits that can be spotted near the campus are cute.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Rabbit x ∧ Spotted x → Cute x
-- Some turtles can be spotted near the campus.
axiom A2 : ∃ x : obj, Turtle x ∧ Spotted x
-- The only animals that can be spotted near the campus are rabbits and squirrels.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Spotted x → (Rabbit x ∨ Squirrel x)
-- If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬Calm x
-- All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Squirrel x ∧ Spotted x → Skittish x
-- Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom A6 : Spotted Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie

-- Derive: Rockie is either cute or skittish (unconditionally).
theorem cute_or_skittish_Rockie : Cute Rockie ∨ Skittish Rockie := by
  have sp : Spotted Rockie := And.left A6
  have h : Rabbit Rockie ∨ Squirrel Rockie := A3 Rockie sp
  cases h with
  | inl r =>
    apply Or.inl
    have rs : Rabbit Rockie ∧ Spotted Rockie := And.intro r sp
    exact A1 Rockie rs
  | inr s =>
    apply Or.inr
    have ss : Squirrel Rockie ∧ Spotted Rockie := And.intro s sp
    exact A5 Rockie ss

-- The given statement holds: If Rockie is not both a turtle and a squirrel, then Rockie is either cute or skittish.
theorem statement_true :
  (¬(Turtle Rockie ∧ Squirrel Rockie)) → (Cute Rockie ∨ Skittish Rockie) := by
  intro _
  exact cute_or_skittish_Rockie

-- Hence, the negation of the statement cannot hold.
theorem negation_impossible :
  ¬( (¬(Turtle Rockie ∧ Squirrel Rockie)) ∧ ¬(Cute Rockie ∨ Skittish Rockie) ) := by
  intro h
  have notQ : ¬(Cute Rockie ∨ Skittish Rockie) := And.right h
  exact notQ cute_or_skittish_Rockie
</lean>

axiom Near : obj → Prop

-- All rabbits that can be spotted near the campus are cute.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Rabbit x ∧ Near x → Cute x
-- Some turtles can be spotted near the campus.
axiom A2 : ∃ x : obj, Turtle x ∧ Near x

-- If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬Calm x
-- All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Squirrel x ∧ Near x → Skittish x
-- Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom A6 : Near Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie

theorem statement_false :

  have hNear : Near Rockie := And.left A6
  have hCalm : Calm Rockie := And.right A6

    have hNotCalm : ¬ Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hSk

    exact And.left h
